ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location where the observation was made */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the observation */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the observation */,
  "day" INTEGER /* Day of the observation */,
  "t_max" REAL /* Maximum temperature recorded on that day (°C) */,
  "t_min" REAL /* Minimum temperature recorded on that day (°C) */,
  "precip_mm" REAL /* Precipitation measured in millimeters */,
  "t_ave" REAL /* Average temperature for that day (°C) */,
  "precip_cm" REAL /* Precipitation measured in centimeters */,
  "rh_ave" REAL /* Average relative humidity for that day (%) */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Average wind speed for the day (km/h) */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Predominant wind direction */,
  "weather_event" TEXT /* Any significant weather events observed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_records_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., city, region) */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location (meters) */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meteorologists" (
  "meteorologist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each meteorologist */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the meteorologist */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the meteorologist */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliated organization or institution */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the meteorologist */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the meteorologist (e.g., researcher, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("meteorologist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_uploads" (
  "upload_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each upload event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather record being uploaded */,
  "meteorologist_id" INTEGER /* ID of the meteorologist who uploaded the record */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was uploaded */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., manual entry, automated sensor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("upload_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_uploads_meteorologist_id FOREIGN KEY ("meteorologist_id") REFERENCES meteorologists ("meteorologist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reporting" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location for which the report is generated */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date of report generation */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report contents */,
  "report_file" TEXT /* File path to the report document */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., monthly, annual) */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the meteorologist who generated the report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reporting_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES meteorologists ("meteorologist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "climate_zones" (
  "zone_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each climate zone */,
  "zone_name" TEXT /* Name of the climate zone (e.g., Tropical, Temperate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the climate zone characteristics */,
  "average_temp" REAL /* Average temperature range for the climate zone (°C) */,
  "average_precipitation" REAL /* Average precipitation for the climate zone (mm) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("zone_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_climate_zones" (
  "mapping_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "zone_id" INTEGER /* ID of the climate zone */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mapping_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_location_climate_zones_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_location_climate_zones_zone_id FOREIGN KEY ("zone_id") REFERENCES climate_zones ("zone_id")
)
